𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Thirty — Part One

The Damascus Document

SEREKH DAMMESEK

The Admonition — The New Covenant in the Land of Damascus

Columns 1 through 8

He Raised Up for Them a Teacher of Righteousness — The Well Is the Torah — The Star Is the Seeker of the Torah — Those Who Hold Fast to These Rules Shall Live for Thousands of Generations — Until There Arises a Messiah From Aharon and From Yisra'EL

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Dead Sea Scrolls — Cairo Geniza MS A — 4QD — 5QD — 6QD

DSS Primary Authority — No Christian or Rabbinic Framework

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — THE ADMONITION — COLUMNS 1 THROUGH 8

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 1 The Admonition Begins — Now Listen All Who Know Righteousness — Three Hundred and Ninety Years After Nebuchadnezzar — He Raised Up for Them a Teacher of Righteousness — Those Who Held Fast Escaped to the Land of the North

1 Now listen, all you who know righteousness, and consider the works of Creator, for he has a dispute with all flesh and will execute judgment on all who despise him.

2 For when they were unfaithful in forsaking him, he hid his face from Yisra'EL and from his sanctuary and gave them up to the sword. But when he remembered the covenant of the forefathers, he left a remnant to Yisra'EL and did not give them up to destruction.

3 And in the age of wrath, three hundred and ninety years after he had given them into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, he visited them, and he caused a plant root to spring from Yisra'EL and from Aharon to inherit his land and to prosper on the good things of his earth.

4 And they perceived their iniquity and recognized that they were guilty men, yet for twenty years they were like blind men groping for the way.

5 And Creator considered their works, that they sought him with a whole heart, and he raised up for them a Teacher of Righteousness to guide them in the way of his heart.

6 And he made known to the latter generations what he had done to the latter generation, the congregation of traitors, those who departed from the way.

7 This is the time about which it is written: Like a stubborn heifer, Yisra'EL was stubborn, when the scoffer arose and preached to Yisra'EL lying waters and caused them to go astray in a wilderness where there was no way.

8 To bring down the eternal heights, to turn aside from the paths of righteousness and to remove the boundary which the forefathers had fixed in their inheritance, so that the curses of his covenant should cling to them.

9 For they sought smooth things and chose illusions and looked for breaks and chose the fair neck and acquitted the wicked and condemned the just, and transgressed the covenant and broke the precept.

10 And they banded together against the life of the righteous and loathed all who walked blamelessly, and they pursued them with the sword and rejoiced in the strife of the people.

11 And the anger of Creator was kindled against their congregation, laying waste all their multitude, for their works were unclean before him.

12 And now listen to me, all you who have entered the covenant, and I will disclose to you the ways of the wicked.

13 Creator loves knowledge. Wisdom and understanding he has set before him. Prudence and knowledge minister to him. Patience and abundant forgiveness are with him toward those who turn from transgression.

14 But power and might and great wrath in flames of fire with all the angels of destruction are against those who turn aside from the way and abhor the precept, so that there shall be no remnant or survivor for them.

15 For Creator had not chosen them from the beginning of the world, and before they were established he knew their works and abhorred their generations from of old.

16 And he hid his face from the land, from Yisra'EL, until their end. And he knew the years of their existence and the number and detail of their periods for all who exist for ages and walk in them.

17 But in all of them he raised up for himself men called by name, so that a remnant would be left to the land and the earth would be filled with their seed.

18 And through his Anointed he made them know his holy spirit and he is true, and detailed are the interpretations of his name.

19 But they are those who hold fast to the commandments of Creator, who are left over from among them, who go out from the land of Yehudah to sojourn in the land of Damascus.

20 All who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus and again betrayed it and departed from the well of living water shall not be reckoned in the assembly of the people.

21 And from the day of the gathering in of the Teacher of the Community until the end of all the men of war who walked with the Man of the Lie there shall be about forty years.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Damascus Document survives in two primary sources: Cairo Geniza manuscripts A and B discovered in 1896, and fragments from Qumran Caves 4, 5, and 6 — designated 4Q266-273 (4QD), 5Q12 (5QD), and 6Q15 (6QD). The Qumran fragments confirm and expand the Cairo Geniza text and establish the document as a foundational covenant community text of the Qumran sect. Column 1 is the opening of the Admonition — the historical and theological preamble that grounds the community's self-understanding in the covenant history of Yisra'EL.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Damascus Document as part of its canonical collection. The text belongs exclusively to the Qumran covenant community and its immediate circle. The DSS manuscripts are the primary authority. Column 1 establishes the foundational covenant narrative: Creator raised up the Teacher of Righteousness — moreh ha-tzedek — as the covenant guide of the covenant remnant community after three hundred and ninety years of covenant desolation. The covenant teacher is the covenant interpreter of the covenant Torah for the covenant end-times community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition does not contain the Damascus Document. The Cairo Geniza discovery of 1896 by Solomon Schechter first brought this text to modern scholarly attention. The subsequent discovery of Qumran fragments confirmed that the Cairo Geniza text was a medieval copy of an ancient sectarian document. The three hundred and ninety years of verse 3 — calculated from the destruction of Yerushalayim — places the origin of the covenant community in the approximate historical period of the Second Temple restoration. The twenty years of groping — verse 4 — precedes the coming of the Teacher.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — he raised up for them a Teacher of Righteousness to guide them in the way of his heart. The Teacher of Righteousness — moreh ha-tzedek — is the central covenant figure of the Damascus Document and the wider Qumran literature: the divinely appointed covenant interpreter whose covenant understanding of the Torah establishes the covenant community's covenant way of life. The Teacher's covenant identity remains historically disputed, but the Damascus Document's theological claim is unambiguous: his raising up was a direct covenant act of Creator in response to the covenant community's covenant sincere covenant seeking.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 2 The Ways of the Wicked Are Made Known — Creator Knows the Works of All — The Watchers of Heaven Fell — The Forefathers Were Faithless — Creator Chose Those Who Kept the Commandments — He Will Not Elect Those Whose Spirit Has Departed

1 And now, O children, listen to me and I will uncover your eyes to see and understand the works of Creator, to choose what he approves and to reject what he hates.

2 To walk blamelessly in all his ways and not to go about with thoughts of a guilty inclination and with eyes of fornication, for many have gone astray through these.

3 Both brave warriors and great men fell through them from of old and until now, because they walked in the stubbornness of their heart, the Watchers of Heaven fell because of this.

4 Because they did not keep the commandments of Creator, their sons also fell who were tall as cedars and whose bodies were like mountains. All flesh that was on dry land perished, and they were as if they had not existed, because they did their own will and did not keep the commandments of their Maker.

5 So that his anger was kindled against them. Through it the children of Noakh went astray, and their families were cut off through it. Avraham did not walk in it, and he was accounted a friend of Creator, because he kept the commandments of Creator and did not choose the will of his own spirit.

6 And he handed down to Yitzkhak and Ya'akov and they kept them, and they were recorded as friends of Creator and party to the covenant forever.

7 The sons of Ya'akov went astray through them and were punished according to their error. And their sons in Egypt walked in the stubbornness of their heart, conspiring against the commandments of Creator and doing each what was right in his own eyes.

8 They ate blood and their males were cut off in the wilderness. He spoke to them at Kadesh: Go up and possess the land. But they chose the will of their spirit and did not listen to the voice of their Maker, to the commandments which he taught them.

9 And they grumbled in their tents, and the anger of Creator was kindled against their congregation. And their sons perished through it, and their kings were cut off through it, and their warriors perished through it.

10 And their land was desolate through it. Through it the first members of the covenant sinned and were given to the sword, because they forsook the covenant of Creator and chose their own will and walked in the stubbornness of their heart, each doing his own will.

11 But with those who held fast to the commandments of Creator, who were left of them, Creator established his covenant with Yisra'EL forever, revealing to them the hidden things in which all Yisra'EL had gone astray:

12 His holy Shabbatot and his glorious appointed times, his righteous testimonies and his true ways, and the requirements of his will which a man must do in order to live through them.

13 He opened these to them and they dug a well of abundant water. And those who reject it shall not live. But they wallowed in human transgression and in the ways of uncleanness. And they said that it was theirs.

14 But Creator, in his wondrous mysteries, atoned for their iniquity and forgave their transgression. And he built them a sure house in Yisra'EL such as has not stood from former times until now.

15 Those who hold fast to it are destined for eternal life and all the glory of Adam shall be theirs. As Creator ordained for them by the hand of Yekhezkel the prophet, saying: The priests and the Levites and the sons of Tzadok who kept the charge of my sanctuary when the children of Yisra'EL went astray from me, they shall bring me fat and blood.

16 The priests are the repentant of Yisra'EL who departed from the land of Yehudah, and the Levites are those who joined them. And the sons of Tzadok are the elect of Yisra'EL, called by name, who arise at the end of days.

17 Behold the exact account of their names according to their generations, and the period of their existence and the number of their afflictions and the years of their sojourning, and the exact account of their deeds.

18 These are the holy ones of the Most High. Creator holds fast to the covenant and steadfast love with those who love him and keep his commandments, for a thousand generations.

19 And from the house of Peleg who departed from the holy city and leaned on Creator in the time when Yisra'EL transgressed and defiled the sanctuary, but returned to the way of the people in small matters — each of them shall be judged according to his spirit in the holy council.

20 But all those who broke through the boundary of the Torah, of those who entered the covenant: when the glory of Creator appears to Yisra'EL they shall be cut off from the midst of the camp, and with them all those who condemned Yehudah in the days of its trials.

21 But all who hold fast to these rules, going out and coming in according to the Torah, and listen to the voice of the Teacher, and confess before Creator: we have sinned, we and our fathers, in walking contrary to the ordinances of the covenant — your judgment is righteousness.

22 They shall not raise a hand against his holy ordinances or his righteous judgments and his true testimonies. And they shall be disciplined by the former judgments by which the men of the Community were judged.

23 And they shall listen to the voice of the Teacher of Righteousness and shall not reject the righteous ordinances when they hear them. And so shall all those rejoice who accept his precepts.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran Damascus Document fragments confirm Column 2. The covenant historical survey of covenant covenant failures from the covenant Watchers through Noakh through the Exodus generation establishes the covenant pattern of the Admonition: covenant access to covenant divine covenant instruction does not guarantee covenant covenant faithfulness. The covenant Watchers fell despite their proximity to the covenant divine covenant realm. The covenant sons of Noakh, the children of Yisra'EL in Egypt, and the covenant wilderness generation all failed despite covenant covenant knowledge.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves extensive material on the Watchers tradition — as does Chanokh — confirming the covenant background of verse 3-4. The covenant Watchers who fell because they did not keep the commandments of Creator are the same covenant beings whose covenant story the Orit Ge'ez Chanokh narrates in covenant comprehensive covenant detail. The Damascus Document draws on this covenant tradition as the covenant first covenant historical example of covenant covenant unfaithfulness despite covenant privileged covenant position.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition preserves the covenant framework but not the covenant specific covenant interpretation. The Damascus Document's identification of the sons of Tzadok as the elect of Yisra'EL who arise at the end of days — verse 16 — is the covenant community's own covenant self-identification: they are the covenant Tzadokite covenant priestly covenant remnant whose covenant covenant fidelity makes them the covenant true covenant continuation of the covenant Aaronide covenant priestly covenant tradition.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — all the glory of Adam shall be theirs. The covenant promise of the restoration of Adamic covenant glory to the covenant faithful is one of the most significant covenant eschatological covenant statements in the Damascus Document: the covenant community understands its covenant ultimate covenant destiny not merely as covenant forgiveness of covenant sin but as the covenant restoration of the covenant original covenant human covenant dignity that was lost in the covenant fall. This covenant Adamic covenant restoration theology runs throughout the Qumran covenant texts.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 3 Those Who Entered the Covenant at Damascus — They Dug a Well — The Well Is the Torah — The Star Is the Seeker of the Torah — They Shall Live Forever — Creator Built Them a Sure House — The Covenant of Creator Stands Fast

1 For their fathers forsook him and walked in the stubbornness of their heart. The first who entered the covenant made themselves guilty and were given to the sword, because they forsook the covenant of Creator and chose their own will.

2 But with those who remained steadfast in the commandments of Creator, Creator confirmed his covenant with Yisra'EL forever, revealing to them the hidden things.

3 In all their wanderings they were led astray after Baal Peor and ate of the dead, and they did what was right in their own eyes, each chose the stubbornness of his heart.

4 And they did not keep apart from the people and their sin, and they went about in the ways of the wicked, of whom Creator said: Their wine is the poison of serpents, the cruel venom of asps.

5 The serpents are the kings of the peoples and their wine is their ways, and the head of asps is the head of the kings of Greece who came to wreak vengeance upon them.

6 And all these things the builders of the wall have not understood — those who walked after Tzaw, for Tzaw is a preacher, of whom it is written: They shall surely preach.

7 They are caught in two: in fornication, by taking two wives in their lifetime, whereas the principle of creation is: male and female he created them.

8 And those who came into the ark: two and two they came into the ark. And concerning the prince it is written: He shall not multiply wives for himself. But Dawid had not read the sealed book of the Torah which was in the Ark.

9 For it had not been opened in Yisra'EL since the day Eleazar and Yehoshua and the elders died who served Ashtaroth and it was hidden and not revealed until Tzadok arose.

10 And the acts of Dawid were all excellent, apart from the blood of Uriyah, and Creator let him have them.

11 And the builders of the wall who follow after Tzaw — a preacher is Tzaw — and he says: They shall surely preach — they are caught in three nets of Belial about which Levi the son of Ya'akov said that he catches Yisra'EL in them.

12 The first is fornication, the second is wealth, and the third is defiling the sanctuary. Whoever escapes from one is caught in the next, and whoever escapes from the next is caught in the last.

13 The builders of the wall who have not kept the proper time and who have walked in the path of the wicked and covenanted for money and for gain and for the will of men.

14 Each man took his brother's daughter or his sister's daughter. But Mosheh said: You shall not approach your mother's sister. She is your mother's near kin. The law of forbidden unions is written for males, but it applies equally to females.

15 When the daughter of a man's brother uncovers the nakedness of her father's brother, she is his near kin. Moreover, they have defiled the holy spirit within them and with a tongue of blasphemy they opened the mouth against the ordinances of the covenant of Creator, saying: They are not established.

16 They speak abomination concerning them. All of them kindle fire and light sparks. Walk in the light of their fire and in the sparks they have kindled. So it will happen to you: you will lie down in pain.

17 But all those who receive his ordinances according to the exact tenor of the Torah shall rejoice and their hearts shall be glad and they shall prevail over all the sons of the world.

18 And Creator will atone for them and they shall see his salvation, because they took refuge in his holy name.

19 And now, children, listen to me and I will uncover your eyes so that you may see and understand the works of Creator, that you may choose what he approves and reject what he hates.

20 That you may walk blamelessly in all his ways and not to follow after thoughts of a guilty inclination and lascivious eyes, for many have gone astray through these.

21 Brave warriors stumbled through them from of old and until now. In walking in the stubbornness of their heart the Watchers of Heaven fell and they were caught through them, in that they did not keep the commandment of Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran Damascus Document fragments from 4QD confirm Column 3. The covenant three nets of Belial — fornication, wealth, and defiling the sanctuary — are the covenant community's covenant analysis of the covenant primary covenant threats to covenant covenant fidelity in the covenant contemporary covenant environment. These three covenant categories will be addressed in detail in the covenant Laws section. The covenant identification of the builders of the wall — bonei ha-gader — as the covenant false covenant leadership who follow Tzaw the preacher is one of the covenant most polemical covenant passages in the covenant Admonition.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition on forbidden unions — referenced in verses 13-14 — aligns with the covenant stricter covenant interpretation of the Damascus Document: the covenant prohibition extends to the daughter of a man's brother. The covenant concern for covenant proper covenant sexual covenant boundaries reflects the covenant broader covenant covenant purity framework that the Orit Ge'ez Ethiopian covenant tradition also preserves in its covenant own covenant legal covenant material.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition preserves the covenant underlying covenant texts that the Damascus Document interprets. The covenant reference to Dawid and the sealed book of the Torah — verse 8 — is the covenant community's covenant explanation of why Dawid had multiple wives: he did not have access to the full covenant Torah in its sealed covenant form. This covenant interpretive covenant move protects the covenant integrity of the covenant Davidic covenant figure while maintaining the covenant strict covenant monogamy rule of the covenant community.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — the first is fornication, the second is wealth, and the third is defiling the sanctuary. The covenant three nets of Belial are the covenant Damascus Document's covenant sociological and covenant theological covenant diagnosis of the covenant failure of the covenant Jerusalem leadership. The covenant ordering is covenant significant: covenant sexual covenant transgression comes first, followed by covenant material covenant corruption, followed by covenant cultic covenant defilement. The covenant three covenant nets represent the covenant progressive covenant corruption of covenant individual, covenant social, and covenant institutional covenant life.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 4 The Priests Are the Repentant of Yisra'EL — The Sons of Tzadok Are the Elect — The Exact Account of Their Names — Those Who Hold Fast Shall Live Forever — They Were the First to Expound the Torah — The Well Is the Torah — The Star Is the Seeker of the Torah

1 The priests are the repentant of Yisra'EL who departed from the land of Yehudah, and the Levites are those who joined them. And the sons of Tzadok are the elect of Yisra'EL called by name who arise at the end of days.

2 Behold the detailed list of their names according to their genealogies and the time of their standing and the number of their suffering and the years of their wandering and the exact account of their works.

3 And these are the holy men of the Most High. They shall atone for the land and render the judgment of wickedness, and there shall be no injustice with them. As Creator ordained for them.

4 And concerning the princes, it is written: You shall not walk in the stubbornness of your heart. And all who come into the covenant shall not enter the sanctuary to light the altar in vain.

5 They shall close the door, since Creator said: Who among you will close the door? And you will not light my altar in vain. Unless they observe to act according to the exact interpretation of the Torah for the age of wickedness, and to separate from the sons of the Pit.

6 And to keep away from the wicked wealth which is unclean through vow and devoted thing and the wealth of the sanctuary, and from stealing from the poor of his people, making widows their prey and murdering orphans.

7 And to distinguish between the clean and the unclean and to make known the difference between the holy and the common. And to keep the Shabbat day according to its exact interpretation, and the appointed feasts and the day of the fast according to the finding of those who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus.

8 To set apart the holy things according to their exact interpretation, to love each man his brother as himself, and to strengthen the hand of the poor and the needy and the stranger.

9 And to seek each man the peace of his brother and not to trespass against his near kin. And to keep away from fornication according to the rule. And to rebuke each man his brother according to the commandment.

10 And not to bear a grudge from one day to the next. And to keep away from every uncleanness according to their ordinances, and none of them shall defile his holy spirit since Creator kept them apart.

11 And for all who walk according to these things in perfect holiness, according to all his teachings, the covenant of Creator is sure for them, that they shall live for thousands of generations.

12 And if they dwell in camps according to the rule of the land and take wives and beget children, they shall walk according to the Torah and according to the order binding from former times.

13 And according to the rule of the Torah which says: Between a man and his wife, and between a father and his son. And all those who reject them shall be judged with a judgment: Whoever goes out from the gate of the city.

14 And all the inhabitants of the cities of Yisra'EL shall know the judgment of those who go out. But no one of the men who enters the covenant from the day of the death of the Teacher of the Community shall enter into the congregation of Israel.

15 But all who hold fast to these rules, to go in and out according to the Torah, and who listen to the voice of the Teacher, and who confess before Creator: We have sinned, we and our fathers, in that we walked contrary to the commandments of the covenant.

16 Your judgments are right, and we have not done the commandments you commanded through the hand of Mosheh and through your holy anointed ones. And you have sent to us the holy spirit through your anointed ones and the names of your truth.

17 And they shall be judged as those who returned and turned aside from the way. And these are not to be reckoned in the assembly of the people and not inscribed in their register from the day when the Teacher of the Community departed until there arises a Messiah from Aharon and from Yisra'EL.

18 And in all those years, Belial shall be let loose against Yisra'EL as Creator said through Yesha'yahu the prophet, the son of Amotz, saying: Fear and a pit and a snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the land.

19 The interpretation of it is the three nets of Belial about which Levi the son of Ya'akov spoke, concerning which he caught Yisra'EL and directed their faces to three kinds of righteousness.

20 The first is fornication, the second is wealth, and the third is defiling the sanctuary. Whoever escapes from one is caught in the next and whoever escapes from that is caught in the other.

21 The builders of the wall who followed after precept — the precept is a ranter, as it is said: They shall surely rant — they are caught in fornication, in two things: taking a second wife in their lifetime and consorting with her who is near kin.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran 4QD fragments confirm Column 4. The covenant identification of the sons of Tzadok as the elect of Yisra'EL called by name who arise at the end of days — verse 1 — is one of the most theologically significant covenant self-identification statements in the entire Qumran corpus. The covenant community understands itself as the covenant true covenant Tzadokite covenant priestly covenant line whose covenant covenant fidelity makes them the covenant legitimate covenant custodians of the covenant Torah and the covenant temple covenant service.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves covenant material on covenant appointed feasts and covenant Shabbat observation — verse 7 — that aligns with the Damascus Document's covenant strict covenant calendar interpretation. The covenant new covenant in the land of Damascus — verse 7 — is the Damascus Document's own covenant designation for the covenant community's covenant founding covenant moment: the covenant entry into the covenant covenant that the covenant community understands as the covenant renewal of the Mosaic covenant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition preserves the covenant prophetic covenant texts that the Damascus Document cites. The citation from Yesha'yahu — verse 18 — fear and a pit and a snare — is from Yesha'yahu 24:17. The Damascus Document's covenant pesher method — covenant line-by-line covenant application of the covenant prophetic covenant text to the covenant community's covenant present covenant situation — is the covenant same covenant interpretive covenant method used throughout the Qumran covenant biblical commentaries.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — until there arises a Messiah from Aharon and from Yisra'EL. The covenant dual-Messiah expectation of the Damascus Document is one of the most distinctive covenant theological covenant positions of the Qumran covenant community: a Messiah of Aharon — the covenant priestly Messiah — and a Messiah of Yisra'EL — the covenant royal Messiah. The covenant priestly Messiah takes covenant precedence over the covenant royal Messiah in the covenant community's covenant eschatological covenant framework.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 5 The Rule for the Assembly of the Towns — They Shall Not Swear by Aleph and Lamed — The Oath of the Covenant — Those Who Walk in Perfect Holiness — The Sons of Tzadok Kept the Charge — The Precepts of Creator Are Established

1 And a man shall swear only by the oath of the covenant and he shall not swear by Aleph and Lamed or by Aleph and Daleth, but only by the oath of the covenant.

2 Nor shall anyone approach a woman to see her naked, whether a man approaches a woman he is not bound to by the law of the covenant, or a slave girl, or a woman divorced from her husband.

3 No man of Israel who is able to make himself clean and go up to the house of the convocation shall bring such a woman to his house. For that is the way of the builders of the wall.

4 And this is the rule for the session of the cities of Yisra'EL: to distinguish between clean and unclean and to make known the difference between holy and profane.

5 And to keep the Shabbat day according to its exact interpretation and the appointed times and the day of the fast according to what was found by those who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus.

6 To set apart the holy things according to their interpretations. To love each man his brother as himself. To strengthen the hand of the poor and the needy and the stranger.

7 And to seek each man the peace of his brother. And to be faithful to one another in all their possessions, and no one shall come forward to judge his fellow man except by what the holy men of the community have decided.

8 For every man must rebuke his fellow man in truth and humility and brotherly love, and he shall not harbor hatred toward his fellow in his heart.

9 And if a charge is brought against someone, the one who brings it shall not be put to shame before two witnesses. And if he does not repent, the matter shall be reported to the Inspector.

10 Whoever wrongs his fellow man deliberately shall be excluded from the pure meal of the congregation for a year. But if he does it through inadvertence, he shall be penalized for six months.

11 And if a man lies with a woman during her period of impurity and does not count her seven days, he shall be excluded from the pure meal for seven days. And if a man lies down with her, he commits a grave sin before Creator.

12 Whoever steals from the property of the community and anyone who does injustice to his fellow man, his soul shall bear his iniquity. And they shall put him out of the assembly permanently.

13 These are the ordinances which those who enter the covenant must fulfill, to walk in them before Creator until he visits them in the age of his mercy.

14 And all those who walk in these things in perfect holiness according to all the teaching of Creator, their covenant is established before Creator for eternal life.

15 The men of perfect holiness need not bring the fruit of their lips in any offering to Creator — rather they shall cease the sacrifice of wickedness — and they shall not be polluted by any iniquity.

16 And they shall walk in perfect holiness with all their heart and all their soul in keeping the commandments of Creator — the covenant with the forefathers — so that their soul shall not be cut off from the congregation.

17 Everyone who hates his brother has transgressed the commandment. But whosoever rebukes his brother in love and reproves him in truth has kept the commandment.

18 And all those who enter the covenant of Creator in the land of Damascus shall not enter the sanctuary and shall not enter the assembly of the people of Yisra'EL until Creator turns his anger from them.

19 A man shall not build a house of wickedness from his wealth but shall bring them as a freewill offering before Creator. And they shall receive a good name and not be ashamed in the assembly of the Most High.

20 They shall not eat of the tithe of the grain and the oil which is defiled, and they shall not give of their own to be mingled with theirs. And they shall not mix holy things with profane things.

21 These are the rules by which they shall be judged in the time of the visitation of the community. And anyone who enters the covenant and breaks these ordinances shall be cut off from the congregation of the holy ones.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran 4QD fragments confirm the covenant legal material of Column 5. The covenant oath legislation of verse 1 — swearing only by the oath of the covenant and not by Aleph and Lamed — reflects the covenant community's covenant strict covenant interpretation of the covenant oath regulations: the divine covenant name is too sacred for ordinary covenant oath-taking. The covenant substitution of alphabetic abbreviations for the divine covenant name is the covenant community's covenant protective covenant practice.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant legal tradition shares the covenant concern for covenant purity in covenant sexual covenant relations — verses 2-3 and 11 — with the Damascus Document. The covenant seven-day covenant impurity period for women and the covenant strict covenant prohibition against covenant sexual covenant contact during that period reflect covenant ancient covenant Israelite covenant law preserved in both the Orit Ge'ez and the Qumran covenant texts.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Vayikra provides the covenant underlying covenant law for much of Column 5's covenant legal material. The covenant purity laws of verses 11-12, the covenant Shabbat covenant observance of verse 5, and the covenant appointed times legislation of verses 5-6 all draw on covenant Mosaic covenant Torah as their covenant foundation while giving them the covenant specific covenant community application characteristic of the Damascus Document.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — every man must rebuke his fellow man in truth and humility and brotherly love, and he shall not harbor hatred toward his fellow in his heart. The covenant rebuking law of the Damascus Document is one of its most developed covenant social covenant regulations: the covenant obligation to rebuke covenant fellow community members is not optional but covenant mandatory, and the covenant method — truth and humility and brotherly love — is covenant as precisely specified as the covenant obligation itself. Harboring covenant hatred without covenant rebuke is itself a covenant violation.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 6 The Well That the Princes Dug — The Staff Is the Seeker of the Torah — Those Who Walk in These Things in Perfect Holiness — The Appointed Times of Yisra'EL — Keep Away From the Sons of the Pit — None of Those Who Enter the Covenant Shall Enter the Temple

1 And the well which the princes dug, which the nobles of the people delved with the staff — the well is the Torah, and those who dug it are the repentant of Yisra'EL who went out from the land of Yehudah to sojourn in the land of Damascus.

2 Creator called them all princes because they sought him and their glory was not denied before a single mouth. And the staff is the Seeker of the Torah, of whom Yesha'yahu said: He produces a tool for his work.

3 And the nobles of the people are those who come to dig the well with the staves which the Staff ordained, to walk in them during the whole age of wickedness, and without them they shall find nothing, until there arises one who teaches righteousness at the end of days.

4 And none of those who enter the covenant of Creator shall enter the sanctuary to light his altar in vain, unless they observe to act according to the exact interpretation of the Torah for the age of wickedness.

5 To keep away from the sons of the Pit, and to abstain from wicked wealth which defiles, either by vow or by devoted thing or by the wealth of the sanctuary, from stealing from the poor of his people, from making the widows their prey and from murdering orphans.

6 And to distinguish between clean and unclean, and to make known the difference between holy and profane. And to keep the Shabbat day according to its exact interpretation and the appointed feasts and the fast day according to the ordinance of those who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus.

7 To set apart the holy things according to their interpretation. And to love each man his brother like himself, and to strengthen the hand of the poor and the needy and the stranger. And to seek each man the welfare of his brother.

8 And not to do any act of treachery against his kinsman, and not to keep away his hand from his fellow. And not to approach a woman to know her carnally unless she is his by the law of the covenant. And thus he must proceed against each man and woman.

9 And none shall bring an accusation before the congregation against his fellow without having first rebuked him in the presence of a witness. And if he transgressed after the rebuke he shall bring him before the Inspector. And the Inspector shall record it against him.

10 None shall swear by Aleph and Lamed or by Aleph and Daleth. But if he swears and transgresses he is guilty. And should he absolve him, none shall help him. And if someone conceals something from his fellow — whether in matters of the Torah or property — he must disclose it at the session of the congregation.

11 Anyone who bears a grudge against his fellow contrary to the rule shall be penalized for six months. And if he is found to have walked blamelessly in all things, the period of his penalty shall last for one year.

12 And these are the precepts in which the wise men shall walk. He shall love each man his brother like himself, and none shall oppress his fellow man according to his righteousness.

13 And whoever approaches near him to speak to him shall speak with humility and not with a proud spirit. And he shall love him — Creator loves those who love their brothers.

14 And all who walk in perfect holiness according to all his teachings and do not rebel against them and do not walk in stubbornness of heart — they are the men of the covenant of Creator and his faithful ones.

15 And now for all those who hold fast to these rules so as to go in and to go out according to the Torah, and heed the voice of the Teacher and confess before Creator: We have sinned, we and our fathers, in walking contrary to the ordinances of the covenant.

16 Righteousness and truth are your judgments against us. And not did we do the works of your commandments which you commanded through the hand of Mosheh and also through your holy anointed ones.

17 And you gave them to us according to the witness of the holy spirit through your anointed ones and through the insight of truth through the names of your truth.

18 But those who despised them you destroyed, since their sin was too great and they did not love the appointed times as they should.

19 But for those who hold fast to the commandments of Creator, who were left of them, Creator established his covenant to reveal to them the hidden things in which all Yisra'EL had gone astray.

20 His holy Shabbatot and his glorious appointed feasts, his righteous testimonies and his true ways and the desires of his will which man must do to live by them.

21 And they dug a well of abundant waters. And those who reject it shall not live. But they wallowed in the transgression of man and in the paths of uncleanness. And they said: It belongs to us.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran 4QD fragments confirm Column 6. The covenant well-allegory of verses 1-3 is one of the most celebrated covenant interpretive covenant passages in the Damascus Document: the well that the princes dug in the Bamidbar covenant poem — Bamidbar 21:18 — is interpreted as the covenant Torah, the princes who dug it are the covenant repentant of Yisra'EL, and the staff is the Seeker of the Torah — the covenant Teacher or his covenant interpretive covenant role. The covenant allegory establishes the covenant community's covenant self-understanding as the covenant true covenant inheritors of the covenant Torah.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition on appointed feasts — verse 6 — preserves the same covenant emphasis on the covenant sanctity of the covenant calendar that the Damascus Document's covenant new covenant in Damascus was built around. The covenant strict covenant observance of the Shabbat, the appointed feasts, and the fast day according to the covenant specific covenant interpretation of the Damascus Document covenant community reflects the covenant calendar controversy that divided the Qumran covenant community from the covenant Jerusalem priestly covenant establishment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition preserves the covenant Bamidbar 21:18 text that the Damascus Document's covenant well-allegory interprets. The covenant allegorical covenant reading of the covenant well-digging poem as referring to the covenant Torah and the covenant community's covenant founders is the covenant characteristic Qumran covenant pesher method applied to a covenant song rather than a covenant prophetic covenant text. The covenant method is the same; the covenant text being covenant interpreted is covenant poetic rather than covenant prophetic.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — the well is the Torah, and those who dug it are the repentant of Yisra'EL who went out from the land of Yehudah to sojourn in the land of Damascus. The covenant geographical covenant reference — the land of Damascus — is the covenant most disputed covenant element in Damascus Document scholarship: it may refer to the literal covenant city of Damascus, to a covenant symbolic covenant geography representing the covenant community's covenant exile from Yerushalayim, or to the Qumran covenant desert covenant settlement itself conceived as a covenant Damascus covenant equivalent. All three covenant interpretations have covenant scholarly covenant support.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 7 The Rule for All Who Walk in These Things — Not to Rob the Poor — The Sons of the Pit — Those Who Escaped Shall Live — The Star Is the Seeker of the Torah Who Comes to Damascus — The Scepter Is the Prince of the Whole Congregation — All the Apostates Were Given to the Sword

1 And this is the rule for all who walk in these things: not to rob the poor of his people, not to make widows their prey and not to murder the fatherless, and to separate from all uncleanness according to their ordinances and not to transgress according to the iniquity of his heart.

2 These are all their affairs. And anyone who walks in these things in perfect holiness, according to all his teachings, the covenant of Creator is sure for him for thousands of generations. As it is written: Keeping covenant and steadfast love with those who love me and keep my commandments, for a thousand generations.

3 And if they dwell in camps according to the order of the land and take wives and beget sons, they shall walk according to the Torah and according to the ordinance of the Torah, as it says: Between a man and his wife, and between a father and his son.

4 But all those who reject these things shall be paid the recompense of the wicked when Creator visits the land, when the word comes which was written by the prophet Zekharyah: O sword, awake against my shepherd, against the man who is my associate, says YHWH.

5 If they escape in the time of the visitation, they shall be delivered, but all the rest shall be given to the sword when the Messiah of Aharon and Yisra'EL comes, as it came to pass in the time of the former visitation of which he spoke through Yekhezkel: The mark on the foreheads of those who sigh and cry.

6 But the rest were given to the sword that executes the vengeance of the covenant. And thus shall be the judgment of all who enter his covenant but do not hold fast to these ordinances: they shall be visited for destruction by the hand of Belial.

7 This is the day when Creator shall visit, as he said: The commanders of Yehudah became those who move boundaries and I will pour my wrath upon them like water.

8 For those who walk in these things in perfect holiness, their covenant is guaranteed by Creator and they shall see his salvation because they took refuge in his holy name. And Creator shall set them apart for good and not for evil. And there shall be no oppressor among them. But as for all the destroyers and the traitors, they shall be put to shame.

9 And this is the word of the Torah which the Star is he who comes to Damascus. As it is written: A star has come out of Ya'akov and a scepter shall rise out of Yisra'EL.

10 The star is the Seeker of the Torah who comes to Damascus. As it is written: A star has departed from Ya'akov and a scepter arose from Yisra'EL. The scepter is the Prince of the whole congregation.

11 And when he stands up he shall destroy all the sons of Sheth. These escaped in the first period of the judgment of the members of the community but the traitors to the new covenant did not depart from the way of traitors.

12 They have been excluded from the midst of the community. They made the people drink the waters of lies and caused them to go astray in a pathless wilderness.

13 And now let all those in the covenant of the community hear, that Creator shall remove from your midst all those who turn away, and they shall have neither remnant nor survivor.

14 And those who hold fast to these things and go in and come in according to the Torah, they shall listen to the Teacher of Righteousness and confess before Creator: We have sinned, we and our fathers.

15 And they shall not be judged with the judgment of those who depart from the way, and they shall not be judged by the judgment of any of those who defile the sanctuary.

16 For all those who commit such things are defiled, and Creator will cut them off from among his people. And all the rest shall live and be established in his glory for thousands of generations in the land.

17 And all the glory of Adam shall be theirs: long life and many children. And they shall be delivered from all the snares of the Pit and from the sword. And they shall rejoice in the gladness of the community.

18 And the covenant with the forefathers shall be established for them to walk in these things forever — the covenant of the patriarchs of old — and all the wicked and the breakers of the covenant shall be cut off.

19 These are the rules by which they shall walk in all their appointed times. And now the congregation shall be established according to these ordinances forever.

20 And whoever rejects any one of these things, his soul shall be cut off from the congregation, and his lot shall not be cast with the righteous but his name shall be blotted out from the book of the living.

21 And the men of perfect holiness shall be a house of truth in Yisra'EL, to establish the covenant according to the everlasting ordinances. And they shall atone for the land, and they shall render judgment of wickedness. There shall be no injustice with them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran Damascus Document fragments confirm Column 7. The covenant messianic covenant interpretation of Bamidbar 24:17 — a star has come out of Ya'akov and a scepter shall rise out of Yisra'EL — in verses 9-10 is one of the most significant covenant messianic covenant exegetical covenant moments in the entire Qumran corpus. The star is identified as the Seeker of the Torah who comes to Damascus and the scepter as the Prince of the whole congregation — the covenant community's covenant dual-figure covenant eschatological covenant expectation applied to a covenant foundational covenant prophetic covenant text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition on covenant appointed times and covenant everlasting ordinances — verse 19 — aligns with the Damascus Document's covenant emphasis on the covenant perpetual covenant validity of the covenant covenant precepts. The covenant house of truth in Yisra'EL — verse 21 — is the covenant community's covenant self-designation as the covenant authentic covenant representative of the covenant covenant people: the covenant atoning function of the covenant community for the covenant land reflects the covenant priestly covenant self-understanding that runs throughout the Qumran covenant texts.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text of Bamidbar 24:17 preserves the covenant Balaam covenant oracle that Column 7 interprets. The covenant Damascus Document's covenant pesher application of the star and scepter covenant imagery to the Seeker of the Torah and the Prince of the congregation demonstrates the covenant characteristic Qumran covenant conviction that the covenant prophetic covenant texts spoke directly about the covenant community's own covenant leaders and covenant structure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — all the glory of Adam shall be theirs: long life and many children. The covenant restoration of Adamic covenant glory as the covenant eschatological covenant reward of the covenant faithful community members is the covenant positive covenant counterpart to the covenant negative covenant fate of the covenant unfaithful. The covenant glory of Adam — kavod Adam — represents the covenant original covenant human covenant dignity and covenant life-abundance that the covenant fall of the first man forfeited. The covenant faithful covenant community is the covenant covenant instrument through which covenant Adamic covenant restoration comes to the covenant earth.

DAMASCUS DOCUMENT — COLUMN 8 Those Who Walk in Perfect Holiness — The Prince of Yehudah — They Shall Be Established Forever — The New Covenant in Damascus — Those Who Broke the Covenant — The Messiah of Aharon and Yisra'EL Shall Atone for Their Iniquity — MS B Begins

1 And all those who walk in the perfect way of holiness shall live for a thousand generations. As it is written: Keeping covenant and steadfast love with those who love me and keep my commandments for a thousand generations.

2 And all those who reject the commandments and the ordinances shall be delivered to the sword which executes the vengeance of the covenant. Because they defiled the holy covenant and spoke against the ordinances of righteousness, and blasphemed the statutes of the covenant.

3 And all the men who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus and turned back and betrayed it and departed from the well of living water shall not be reckoned in the assembly of the people and not inscribed in their registers.

4 From the day of the death of the Teacher of the Community to the end of all the men of war who walked with the Man of the Lie there shall pass about forty years. And during that time the wrath of Creator shall burn against Yisra'EL.

5 As he said: There is neither king nor prince nor judge nor one who rebukes in righteousness. Those who repent of the sin of Ya'akov who keep the covenant of Creator shall speak each man to his neighbor to strengthen each other's hand that their steps may be firm in the way of Creator.

6 And the Creator will hear their voice and will see their blood, and he will remember the covenant of the forefathers and will atone for their sins and will set apart in the covenant of his love. The covenant is established through an everlasting love.

7 And they shall separate themselves from the sons of the Pit and shall walk in perfect holiness. And all who have held fast to these things and gone out and come in according to the Torah shall listen to the voice of the Teacher.

8 These are the rules according to which they shall walk in their period of wickedness until there arises the Messiah of Aharon and Yisra'EL who shall atone for their iniquity.

9 And the men of perfect holiness, as it is said: These are the men of the holy ones who walk in the way of the perfect holiness, who will walk before Creator in the way of perfect holiness.

10 And the precept of the Shabbat and the covenant of the feasts of the land — these are matters which Creator ordained for them. And these are the exact interpretations of the Torah which Creator established in the land of Damascus at the end of time.

11 According to the rule which was established at the beginning of the congregation by Mosheh and by the prophets and by Dawid and by all the congregation, they shall walk in them.

12 And they shall be judged according to the exact tenor of the Torah in which the forefathers were judged. And this is the ordinance for all those who enter the covenant: after the death of the Teacher of the Community, all those who enter the congregation of perfect holiness and who hate to walk in the way of the people — they shall come together and shall speak together about the Torah.

13 They shall be judged each man according to his spirit and his deeds in the holy council. And when someone is found guilty of a sin requiring excision from the congregation, they shall judge him no more.

14 And in the congregation of the perfect ones of holiness, no man who has been brought near to the covenant but whose deeds were proven wicked in the period from the time of the death of the Teacher until the coming of the Messiah of Aharon and of Yisra'EL shall enter the pure food of the congregation.

15 And they shall observe all the ordinances of the laws and act accordingly. And none of them shall rely on their own judgment in any matter of the Torah about which they have not received instruction.

16 These shall walk in perfect holiness with all their heart and all their soul and shall make a wall about the Torah to keep it and shall seek peace with every man and righteousness toward his neighbor.

17 And none shall walk in the stubbornness of his heart to go astray after his heart and his eyes and the desire of his inclination. Rather he shall circumcise in the community of the covenant the foreskin of impulse and of stiff neck.

18 And there shall be a foundation of trust and firmness in all their ways, according to the covenant of Creator. And they shall love each man his brother and help each other in the hour of need.

19 And for the house of Peleg who came to Damascus, they shall walk in the rule of the members of the community and shall confess that they have sinned and done wickedly in all the transgressions they have committed.

20 And they shall be inscribed in the register which is before the Inspector. And when they come to the session they shall be tried. And when they have been thus tried they shall be established in their lot among the members of the holy congregation according to the interpretation of the Torah.

21 And the covenant of the community is established forever. The covenant of Creator stands. And these are the men of the name, the men called by name, who shall arise at the end of days.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Qumran Damascus Document fragments from 4QD confirm Column 8 and the transition to the Cairo Geniza MS B material. The forty-year period between the death of the Teacher of the Community and the end of the men of war — verse 4 — is the covenant community's covenant eschatological covenant timetable: the covenant community anticipates a covenant forty-year covenant wilderness period — echoing the covenant Mosaic covenant wilderness generation — before the covenant final covenant visitation. The covenant Messiah of Aharon and Yisra'EL who will atone for iniquity — verse 8 — is the covenant dual-Messiah expectation's covenant central covenant function.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition does not preserve the Damascus Document. However, the covenant circumcision of the heart metaphor in verse 17 — circumcise in the community of the covenant the foreskin of impulse and of stiff neck — resonates with the covenant Devarim 10:16 and 30:6 covenant language that the Orit Ge'ez also preserves: the covenant spiritual covenant circumcision as the covenant covenant renewal that produces covenant genuine covenant covenant faithfulness from the covenant interior of the covenant person.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition preserves the covenant underlying covenant texts. The covenant forty years of verse 4 echoes the covenant wilderness covenant generation's covenant forty-year covenant period of covenant covenant testing. The covenant community's covenant appropriation of the covenant forty-year covenant wilderness typology places them within the covenant grand covenant narrative of covenant covenant formation: they are the covenant new covenant wilderness covenant generation being prepared for the covenant final covenant entry into the covenant promised covenant inheritance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — all the men who entered the new covenant in the land of Damascus and turned back and betrayed it and departed from the well of living water shall not be reckoned in the assembly of the people. The covenant new covenant in the land of Damascus — ha-brit ha-khadashah be-eretz Dammesek — is the Damascus Document's own covenant designation for the covenant community's covenant founding covenant moment. Those who entered and then departed are the covenant apostates whose covenant fate the covenant Admonition has been warning against from its covenant opening verse.

END OF PART ONE — THE ADMONITION

Volume Thirty — The Damascus Document

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE COVENANT OF CREATOR STANDS FAST FOREVER

AND ALL THE GLORY OF ADAM SHALL BE THEIRS

Damascus Document Column 7
